{{Infobox location
| name = Sacred Valley
| introduced = Unsouled
}}

''See also: [[Sacred Valley Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Sacred Valley is a mountain-protected paradise that was long considered a secluded and civilized refuge, largely forgotten by the rest of the world. It is characterized by its four holy peaks, which serve as homes to major schools and are sites of mythic significance. The region is home to nearly a million people, including powerful clans like the Wei, and features various cities, towns, and ancient structures such as the [[Ancestor's Tomb]]. A massive, elaborate script formation buried deep underground serves as a protective curse for the valley, though it is also linked to a vast, world-spanning [[labyrinth]] that exists beneath the land. Culturally, the valley is defined by strict traditions, including biannual spiritual testing for children and periodic festivals. Social structures are built around clans, with a strong emphasis on family honor and lineage. The inhabitants practice [[sacred arts]], ranging from those considered "[[Unsouled]]" to highly advanced practitioners. However, the valley's history is marked by conflict between clans and a catastrophic period of destruction caused by approaching cosmic threats. Following major battles involving [[Dreadgod cults]] and powerful entities, the valley's landscape was shattered, leading to a period of reconstruction where diverse architectural styles and populations from the outside world integrated into the surviving settlements.}}

== Unsouled ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Nestled within a mountain range and surrounded by untamed lands inhabited by barbarian nomads, the Sacred Valley is a paradise home to nearly a million people. This region, which many believe contains all settlements, serves as the residence for [[sacred artists]] and is where clans conduct Spiritual Origin tests using a bowl of liquid [[madra]]. While the [[Wei clan]] carved out a section of this wilderness to grow orus fruit, the valley is protected by its terrain, weather, and significant individuals. Although the clans hold a festival every seven years and children can recite the definition of [[sacred arts]] on command, the location faces threats from [[Li Markuth]]'s territorial ambitions and a monstrous creature that once buried the mountains in earth. Ultimately, [[Lindon]] sought to leave this location, which [[Yerin]] considered too soft, despite the potential benefits of staying.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">24</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where clans conduct Spiritual Origin tests for their children.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c1">''Unsouled'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] is the location where orus fruit grows.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c2">''Unsouled'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] is home to almost a million people.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] carved out a section of wilderness in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c3">''Unsouled'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Any child of [[Sacred Valley]] can recite the definition of [[sacred arts]] on command.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c4">''Unsouled'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Sacred Valley]] is the destination for [[Suriel]]'s course to the fated violation.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6">''Unsouled'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The clans of [[Sacred Valley]] hold a festival every seven years.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c8">''Unsouled'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where [[sacred artists]] reside.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c9">''Unsouled'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Li Markuth]] intends to claim the whole of [[Sacred Valley]] as his personal territory.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c10">''Unsouled'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] was destroyed by a monstrous creature that waded through the mountains and buried it in earth.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11">''Unsouled'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] is a place where [[Jade]] is considered the greatest height and anyone below [[Gold]] is considered powerless.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] is a paradise located within a mountain range.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c12">''Unsouled'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] is protected by weather, terrain, inhospitable surrounding regions, ancient legend, and significant individuals.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] is a region that many believe contains all settlements.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c13">''Unsouled'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] is surrounded by wild and untamed land.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c15">''Unsouled'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The land outside [[Sacred Valley]] is inhabited by barbarian nomads who have tamed [[sacred beasts]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location mentioned by [[Suriel]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c16">''Unsouled'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Lindon]] needs to get out of [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location [[Lindon]] marched out of.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c17">''Unsouled'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Yerin]]'s guidance is needed to escape [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Lindon]] considers that staying in [[Sacred Valley]] might have benefits.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Yerin]] believes [[Sacred Valley]] is too soft.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c18">''Unsouled'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Yerin]] is more powerful than any [[Jade]] in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c20">''Unsouled'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]] uses a test involving a bowl of liquid [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c20" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Soulsmith ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Considered civilized when compared to the [[Desolate Wilds]], Sacred Valley serves as a location where medical care is available and [[Elder Whisper's tower]] is situated. While it is the home of the [[Wei clan]] and the place where [[Lindon]] received his education, [[life]] there has not been easy for an [[Unsouled]]. In this region, [[Yerin]] was more capable than anyone else, yet [[Unsouled]] carrying fortunes often became targets for robbery. The valley utilizes shadesilk to trade with the outside world and allows [[Jade]]-stage practitioners to use spirit sensing, though the term [[Unsouled]] holds no meaning beyond its borders. Additionally, a door exists that is connected to the valley, and local badges are notably smaller than [[Eithan]]'s badge.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">13</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where [[Yerin]] was more capable than anyone.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c2">''Soulsmith'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where medical care can be found.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c3">''Soulsmith'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Life in [[Sacred Valley]] had not been easy for an [[Unsouled]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c4">''Soulsmith'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where [[Elder Whisper's tower]] is situated.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c5">''Soulsmith'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where [[Unsouled]] carrying fortunes are targets for robbery.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c8">''Soulsmith'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sacred Valley]] is the place where [[Lindon]] received his education.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sacred Valley]] is considered civilized compared to the [[Desolate Wilds]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c10">''Soulsmith'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sacred Valley]] uses shadesilk for trading with the outside world.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c12">''Soulsmith'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where [[Jade]]-stage practitioners can use spirit sensing.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c13">''Soulsmith'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The term '[[Unsouled]]' has no meaning outside of [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c14">''Soulsmith'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sacred Valley]] is the home of the [[Wei clan]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c16">''Soulsmith'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The door is connected to [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c18">''Soulsmith'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Eithan]]'s badge is larger than badges used in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c19">''Soulsmith'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Blackflame ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Located to the west, the Sacred Valley is a region of towns and cities inhabited by [[Soulsmiths]] and home to various [[Jades]]. While the land surrounding the valley is considered barbaric and untamed, the area itself serves as a site of conflict between the Wei and [[Li]]. The valley is also connected to the [[Arelius family]] and was the location where [[Yerin]] lost her master's voice nearly a year prior, as well as where she once fought a [[Remnant]] of her master. Despite [[Eithan]]'s inability to save the region, a creature is destined to attack the valley. Ultimately, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] departed from the area.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">13</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sacred Valley]] is home to [[Soulsmiths]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c1">''Blackflame'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sacred Valley]] is a place where the Wei and [[Li]] clash.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c5">''Blackflame'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Eithan]] was unable to save [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c6">''Blackflame'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sacred Valley]] has towns and cities.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c8">''Blackflame'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The land outside [[Sacred Valley]] is widely accepted to be untamed and barbaric.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location mentioned in relation to the [[Arelius family]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c9">''Blackflame'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sacred Valley]] lies to the west.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c12">''Blackflame'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Yerin]] previously fought a [[Remnant]] of her master in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c13">''Blackflame'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Yerin]]'s bones would be rotting in [[Sacred Valley]] if she waited to fight.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c13" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A creature is destined to attack [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c17">''Blackflame'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where [[Jades]] reside.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c18">''Blackflame'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Yerin]] lost her master's voice in [[Sacred Valley]] almost a year ago.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c19">''Blackflame'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] left [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c21">''Blackflame'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Located far to the west beyond the [[Desolate Wilds]], the Sacred Valley is recognized as one of the safest locations in the world. This region served as the childhood home for [[Lindon]], marking a place where he was previously situated. Additionally, the valley is responsible for the fabrication of the [[Unsouled]] concept.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">5</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Lindon]] grew up in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c3">''Skysworn'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where [[Lindon]] was previously located.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c7">''Skysworn'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Sacred Valley]] is considered one of the safest places in the world.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c8">''Skysworn'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Sacred Valley]] fabricated the concept of an [[Unsouled]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c12">''Skysworn'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Sacred Valley]] is located far to the west, past the [[Desolate Wilds]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c13">''Skysworn'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Ghostwater ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location [[Lindon]] has been away from for a year and a half.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c3">''Ghostwater'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Lindon]] lived in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c13">''Ghostwater'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The Sacred Valley is a location mentioned by [[Lindon]] that was once shown to him by [[Suriel]]. This region contains various dangerous areas and is noted for being as vast as an entire imperial capital. Due to the influence of [[Samara]]'s Ring, the nights within the valley remain bright.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">5</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The imperial capital might be the size of the entire [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c6">''Underlord'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location [[Lindon]] mentions.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c8">''Underlord'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Sacred Valley]] contains areas that are dangerous.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c12">''Underlord'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Suriel]] showed [[Lindon]] the [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c15">''Underlord'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Sacred Valley]] is a place where night is bright due to [[Samara]]'s Ring.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c22">''Underlord'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Uncrowned ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The people of [[Sacred Valley]] live in [[Akura]] territory.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c15">''Uncrowned'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Dreadgods]] can be allowed into [[Sacred Valley]] by [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c19">''Uncrowned'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Sacred Valley is a location defined by its adherence to ancient traditions and serves as the birthplace of both [[Lindon]] and the [[Bleeding Phoenix]]. Deep within the earth lies a massive script formation that generates a suppression field, which serves to suppress [[madra]] throughout the region. This area also contains the Ancestor’s [[Tomb]] and a hall where sacred instruments were destroyed by time and a curse, though the local people remain so protective that they would strike a [[Jade]] dead for entering their [[labyrinth]]. While the [[Monarchs]] currently decide the [[fate]] of the valley, a [[Dreadgod]] intends to head toward the location upon waking.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">11</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The people of [[Sacred Valley]] would strike a [[Jade]] dead if they stepped into the [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1">''Wintersteel'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The sacred instruments in the hall were destroyed by time and a curse in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where people follow ancient traditions.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Sacred Valley]] contains a massive script formation buried deep within the earth.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The script formation in [[Sacred Valley]] generates a suppression field.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Sacred Valley]] is the birthplace of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Sacred Valley]] suppresses [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Sacred Valley]] contains the Ancestor’s [[Tomb]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[fate]] of [[Sacred Valley]] is being decided by the [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c2">''Wintersteel'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Dreadgod]] intends to head for [[Sacred Valley]] upon waking.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c24">''Wintersteel'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Sacred Valley]] is [[Lindon]]'s birthplace.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c25">''Wintersteel'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Sacred Valley is a territory containing three clans, including the [[Li clan]], and four schools such as the [[Holy Wind School]]. The region is governed by a suppression field that prevents inhabitants from advancing beyond [[Jade]] or reaching [[Archlord]] rank, interferes with contracts, and weakens techniques. As a [[Dreadgod]] approaches, [[Lindon]] seeks to save the valley and evacuate his family from the area he studied as a child. During the ensuing disaster, citizens flee through northern and eastern exits toward the Wilds while [[Lindon]] uses his fortress' cloud to create a ramp for refugees. Ultimately, the [[Wandering Titan]] arrives and destroys Sacred Valley, leaving the area to be resettled one building at a time.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">53</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]] is heading toward [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2">''Bloodline'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] wants to save [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] needs to be safe.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a territory that needs evacuation.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is under threat from an axe-like force.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4">''Bloodline'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is subject to a suppression field that keeps its people from growing beyond [[Jade]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] and his companions are preparing to return to [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location that needs to be made safe.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location surrounded by a suppression field.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c5">''Bloodline'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] has an eastern entrance.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6">''Bloodline'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] has a boundary in front of it.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A great disaster is coming to destroy [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The suppression field of [[Sacred Valley]] can interfere with contracts.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c7">''Bloodline'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location containing three of the most powerful people.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location [[Orthos]] has been in.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] was in [[Sacred Valley]] before leaving.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8">''Bloodline'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] intends to take his family away from [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] has a northern exit.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9">''Bloodline'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location [[Lindon]] studied as a child.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] contains four schools and three clans.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where the [[Li clan]] resides.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10">''Bloodline'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where the characters are currently located.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] contains a curse that leaves people little better than [[Jades]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The guard assumes the visitors are inhabitants of [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11">''Bloodline'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where [[Yerin]]'s [[madra]] had difficulty supporting her body.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location that the [[Wandering Titan]] might pass through.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] contains various [[Paths]] and is subject to a suppressive field.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12">''Bloodline'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] experienced a quake.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c13">''Bloodline'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location that contains the [[Holy Wind School]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c13" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where a fight occurred or will occur.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c14">''Bloodline'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|People are fleeing from the encroaching threat in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15">''Bloodline'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location with an exit and clan buildings.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] uses his fortress' cloud to form a ramp for [[Sacred Valley]] citizens.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|People were flooding out of [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c16">''Bloodline'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where [[shadow madra]] was seen leaping into the air.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] has a field that suppresses [[Monarch]] might.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17">''Bloodline'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Titan]] was being drawn away from [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where wind can tear up trees.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] contains refugees.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Titan]] was seen wading into [[Sacred Valley]] in a vision.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] destroyed [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18">''Bloodline'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wandering Titan]] has come to [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] was re-settled one building at a time.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|People are fleeing [[Sacred Valley]] into the forest.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Techniques thrown into [[Sacred Valley]] are weakened by the suppression field.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19">''Bloodline'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The light over [[Sacred Valley]] darkened as the ring was damaged.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location that [[Yerin]]'s [[madra]] entered.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location [[Mercy]] intends to fly back to.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Advancing to [[Archlord]] cannot be done in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Titan]] has been in [[Sacred Valley]] for a long time.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c20">''Bloodline'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location near a cloud fortress where [[Eithan]] and [[Yerin]] are located.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c21">''Bloodline'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Jai Chen]] did not want to return to [[Sacred Valley]] in her current condition.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The crowd from [[Sacred Valley]] was ushered into the Wilds.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c21" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Located near [[Mount Samara]], the Sacred Valley serves as the largest hub of the western labyrinth and contains various pieces of the [[labyrinth]] itself. Following a rampage by the [[Dreadgod]] that left the land littered with [[Remnants]], residents sought shelter in the town of [[Refuge]] or established a camp within the [[Desolate Wilds]]. The valley, which exerts a suppression on spiritual sense and is surrounded by four cults, became a site of intense conflict as [[Overlords]] and Archlords fought over the territory. During these clashes of power, emergency gatekeys were used to cordon off the area, and a wave of power gushed out from the valley. The location is set to change once the [[Silent Core]] is placed in its chamber, and it remains a dangerous place where one could die.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">26</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] previously used a rust-red [[Thousand-Mile Cloud]] when leaving [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2">''Reaper'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sacred Valley]] is a place that [[Lindon]] feels has little left to defend.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sacred Valley]] exerts a suppression on spiritual sense.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c3">''Reaper'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Residents of [[Sacred Valley]] found shelter in the town of [[Refuge]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4">''Reaper'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Refugees from [[Sacred Valley]] are using a camp in the [[Desolate Wilds]] for shelter.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Eithan]] might fly to [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c5">''Reaper'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The people of [[Sacred Valley]] are being loaded onto cloudships.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c6">''Reaper'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sacred Valley]] was littered with [[Remnants]] after the [[Dreadgod]]'s rampage.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] has not had much time to relax since leaving [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c7">''Reaper'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Yerin Arelius]] is expected to return to the Valley.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] and [[Eithan]] are hundreds of miles from [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c8">''Reaper'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location that will change when the [[Silent Core]] is placed in its chamber.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A wave of power gushed out of [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Emergency gatekeys are being used to cordon off [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c9">''Reaper'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sacred Valley]] is a land that contains the largest hub of the western labyrinth.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]]'s perception is clearest in [[Sacred Valley]] when the suppression field is gone.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10">''Reaper'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Malice]] was within sight of [[Sacred Valley]] after stepping out of the [[Way]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sacred Valley]] is surrounded by four cults.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The people of [[Sacred Valley]] are associated with the [[Jai]] family.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c11">''Reaper'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Jai Long]] realized they were heading back to [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Sacred Valley]] has badges with the [[Forger symbol]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c12">''Reaper'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Sacred Valley]] is located near [[Mount Samara]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c19">''Reaper'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Overlords]] and Archlords are fighting over [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c21">''Reaper'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location where powers are clashing.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24">''Reaper'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Some pieces of the [[labyrinth]] are located in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c25">''Reaper'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sacred Valley]] is a place where one could die.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26">''Reaper'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The center of Sacred Valley was once leveled by the destructive path of a rampaging [[Titan]], an entity that [[Mercy]] has previously fought within the region. A [[labyrinth]] lies hidden beneath the valley's surface, while [[Northstrider]]'s cloud fortress remains suspended in the sky above it. Although [[Lindon]] and [[Dross]] once felt a great distance from the area, [[Lindon]] remains unable to create a portal to the valley from [[Serpent's Grave]]. Within this location, specific social customs are observed, such as the manners utilized by [[Yerin]].}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">7</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[labyrinth]] is located beneath [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c2">''Dreadgod'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Sacred Valley]]'s center was leveled by a rampaging [[Titan]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Northstrider]]'s cloud fortress hangs over [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c4">''Dreadgod'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] and [[Dross]] felt far from [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6">''Dreadgod'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Mercy]] has fought the [[Titan]] in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location that [[Lindon]] cannot yet create a portal to from Serpent’s [[Grave]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21">''Dreadgod'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Yerin]] uses [[Sacred Valley]] manners.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c22">''Dreadgod'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Located far above the [[labyrinth]] and below the sky, Sacred Valley is a territory situated far from [[Blackflame City]] that contains three remaining sacred peaks. This ancestral home of [[Lindon]]'s people is protected by a great script and a barrier projected by the great [[labyrinth]], though it can also rise from the sea. During a period of intense conflict, [[Lindon]] returned to the valley via the [[labyrinth]] and hovered over the area as it was targeted by [[Monarch]] techniques. The location was pincered by a star and a shining blood [[dragon]] while the gray-white echoed [[Titan]] clashed above it, forcing [[Lindon]] and [[Malice]] to consider retreating if [[Northstrider]] emerged victorious. Following these events, the valley has undergone a rebuilding effort and remains a site of interest regarding its [[Spiritual Origin test]].}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">24</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location [[Reigan Shen]] reflected upon.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c4">''Waybound'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|There is a barrier around [[Sacred Valley]] projected by the great [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] contains three remaining sacred peaks.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c5">''Waybound'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is protected by a great script.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c5" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] returned to [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] was hovering over [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c9">''Waybound'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is located below the sky.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location being targeted by [[Monarch]] techniques.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Akura Charity]] was outside [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c10">''Waybound'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] has a southern entrance near a mountain with a red river.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Archlords can fly around [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] was pincered by a shining blood [[dragon]] and a star.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c11">''Waybound'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location [[Lindon]] and [[Malice]] must back away from if [[Northstrider]] wins.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location that [[Suriel]] claimed [[Lindon]] could level on his own.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c12">''Waybound'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is a territory [[Lindon]] visited via the [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c14">''Waybound'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is the location of [[Lindon]]'s ancestors.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The gray-white echoed [[Titan]] clashed over [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c15">''Waybound'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is far from [[Blackflame City]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c18">''Waybound'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is undergoing a rebuilding effort.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32">''Waybound'', chapter 32</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A [[Sacred Valley]] gesture involves pressing fists against one another and bowing over them.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is located far above the [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c34">''Waybound'', chapter 34</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Sacred Valley]] is a location that can rise from the sea.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c35">''Waybound'', chapter 35</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Li Markuth]] believes all clans of [[Sacred Valley]] will soon belong to him.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c35" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Spiritual Origin test]] of [[Sacred Valley]] is the subject of an information request.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c39">''Waybound'', chapter 39</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Kazan]] and [[Li]] clans are no longer among the top ten families in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] flies into [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Only nomads,” he said dismissively. “Barbarians. They have tamed some sacred beasts, and they roam around in caravans avoiding the greatest dangers.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c15" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|"I need to get out of Sacred Valley, and you need to get away from Heaven’s Glory."<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|If I waited to fight until I was ready,” Yerin said, “my bones would be rotting in Sacred Valley right now.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c13" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|“Sacred Valley,” Lindon said. “It’s far to the west, past the Desolate Wilds.”<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c13" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The sacred instruments that had filled this hall had been destroyed by time and by this valley’s “curse.”<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:Unsouled]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
[[Category:Blackflame]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle locations]]
[[Category:Regions]]
